hypothesis: Round 53 answered only the upward side of the re-opened LR question on the Hadamard-strengthened `gated` anchor: `LR=2.4e-3` stayed healthy but finished slightly worse than the current `0.142923` best, so the remaining single question is whether the new preprocess actually wants a smaller step size instead of a larger one. With `ARCHITECTURE=gated`, `K=128`, `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, and `USE_HADAMARD=1` fixed, does lowering `LR` from `2.0e-3` to `1.6e-3` improve the full curve, or does `2.0e-3` remain the best shared mainline setting even after preprocess changed conditioning?
summary: Stay on the active `gated` mainline and keep the family reference recipe fixed at `ARCHITECTURE=gated`, `K=128`, `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, `AUXK_ALPHA=0.03125`, and `USE_HADAMARD=1`. Change only `LR` from `2.0e-3` to `1.6e-3` to complete the downward side of the LR localization around the current Hadamard anchor. Judge this run by the whole trajectory, not just the endpoint: if the lower LR yields a smoother mid/late descent and finishes below `0.142923`, then Hadamard shifted the preferred step size downward; if it is flatter or worse, lock `2.0e-3` as the operative mainline LR and stop the LR branch again.
self_review: This stays on the same family, respects the fixed `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12` rule, and changes exactly one primary variable: `lr`, anchored on the current family reference point so it remains policy-clean. It also follows the requested mainline sequencing better than opening a new family or retuning K again: after the Hadamard-enabled anchor rejected the upward LR callback at `2.4e-3`, the most informative remaining single question is the symmetric downward callback before declaring the LR axis settled.
notes_to_memory: Round 53 showed that on the Hadamard-enabled `gated@128` anchor, raising `LR` to `2.4e-3` does not improve over `LR=2.0e-3`; the curve stayed healthy but the endpoint regressed slightly to `0.143811` versus the `0.142923` anchor.
Round 54 should isolate the remaining LR question on the same strengthened anchor by testing the downward callback to `LR=1.6e-3` at fixed `gated`, `K=128`, `EF=12`, `signum`, and `USE_HADAMARD=1`.
next_hypotheses: If `LR=1.6e-3` improves the Hadamard-enabled `gated@128` curve or endpoint, adopt it as the new anchor or run one tight confirmation before revisiting other axes.
If `LR=1.6e-3` is also worse or uniformly slower, close the LR branch and keep `LR=2.0e-3` as the shared mainline recipe before considering any further family-local recipe work.
